onChange.script:
# Get the current value type.
	type = self.params.currentParams.type
		
	# Get the current ac (ahu).
	ac = currentValue.value
	
	# Set type to 'other' if ac (ahu) is 3.
	if ac == 3:
		type = 'other'

	# Iterate through the tag 'options' custom property,
	# finding the appropriate tags based on the selected
	# type and selected ac (ahu) and appending these tags
	# to the tags list.
	tags = []
	options = self.custom.options
	for option in options:
		typeProp = option['type']
		acProp = option['ac']
		if typeProp == type and acProp == ac:
			name = option['name']
			units = option['units']
			path = option['path']
			mappings = option['mappings']
			numberFormat = option['numberFormat']
			editable = option['editable']
			
			tags.append({'value':path,'label':name,'units':units,'mappings':mappings,'numberFormat':numberFormat,'editable':editable})
	
	# Set the options for the tag dropdown equal to the tags list.
	self.getChild("root").getChild("Tag").getChild("Tag").props.options = tags

# Get the current value type.
	type = currentValue.value
	
	# Get the current ac (ahu).
	ac = self.params.currentParams.ac

	# Iterate through the tag 'options' custom property,
	# finding the appropriate tags based on the selected
	# type and selected ac (ahu) and appending these tags
	# to the tags list.
	tags = []
	options = self.custom.options
	for option in options:
		typeProp = option['type']
		acProp = option['ac']
		if typeProp == type and acProp == ac:
			name = option['name']
			units = option['units']
			path = option['path']
			mappings = option['mappings']
			numberFormat = option['numberFormat']
			editable = option['editable']
			
			tags.append({'value':path,'label':name,'units':units,'mappings':mappings,'numberFormat':numberFormat,'editable':editable})
	
	# Set the options for the tag dropdown equal to the tags list.
	self.getChild("root").getChild("Tag").getChild("Tag").props.options = tags

transforms.code:
# If the value type is condenser and the user has not manually
	# selected an icon, or if the user manually selected the condenser,
	# then display the condenser icon.
	
	icon = value['icon']
	type = value['type']
	
	if len(icon) == 0 and type=='condenser':
		return True
	elif icon == 'condenser':
		return True
	else:
		return False

# If the value type is compressor and the user has not manually
	# selected an icon, or if the user manually selected the compressor,
	# then display the compressor icon.	
	
	icon = value['icon']
	type = value['type']
	
	if len(icon) == 0 and type=='compressor':
		return True
	elif icon == 'compressor':
		return True
	else:
		return False

# If the value type is fan and the user has not manually
	# selected an icon, or if the user manually selected the fan,
	# then display the fan icon.
	
	icon = value['icon']
	type = value['type']
	
	if len(icon) == 0 and type=='fan':
		return True
	elif icon == 'fan':
		return True
	else:
		return False

# If the value type is heatingControls and the user has not manually
	# selected an icon, or if the user manually selected the heatingControls,
	# icon, then display the heatingControls icon.	
	
	icon = value['icon']
	type = value['type']
	
	if len(icon) == 0 and type=='heatingControls':
		return True
	elif icon == 'heating_controls':
		return True
	else:
		return False

# If the value type is economizer and the user has not manually
	# selected an icon, or if the user manually selected the economizer,
	# then display the economizer icon.
	
	icon = value['icon']
	type = value['type']
	
	if len(icon) == 0 and type=='economizer':
		return True
	elif icon == 'economizer':
		return True
	else:
		return False

config.script:
# Open the 'change icon' popup to give the user the ability to select an icon.
	
	system.perspective.openPopup('change-icon','Building Automation Demo/Page/Systems Overview/Framework/Dashboard/Widgets/Single Status/Framework/Icon Selector/Change Icon', params = {'material':self.view.params.currentParams.icon},modal=True,showCloseIcon=False,draggable=False)

customMethods.name: updateParams
updateParams

onChange.script:
# The user has selected a tag. Utilize the selected tag path to find the 
	# tag in the options list, grab the tag properties, and set the params
	# equal to the tag properties.	
	
	if origin != 'Binding':
		selectedPath = currentValue.value
		options = self.props.options
		
		for option in options:
			if option['value'] == selectedPath:
				label = option['label']
				units = option['units']
				mappings = option['mappings']
				numberFormat = option['numberFormat']
				editable = option['editable']
				
				self.view.params.currentParams.name = label
				self.view.params.currentParams.units = units
				self.view.params.currentParams.mappings = mappings
				self.view.params.currentParams.numberFormat = numberFormat
				self.view.params.currentParams.editable = editable
				
				break

transforms.code:
# Only display the tag dropdown if the AC (AHU) param is equal to 1 or 2 and
	# the type param is greater than 0, or if the AC (AHU) param is equal to 3
	# (other).
	
	ac = value['ac']
	type = value['type']
	
	if ac in [1,2] and type > 0:
		return True
	elif ac == 3:
		return True
	else:
		return False

# If lowThreshold is not a number, then lowThreshold is not being used,
	# so display this button (allows user to utilize a lowThreshold).	
	
	import numbers
		
	if isinstance(value,numbers.Number):
		return False
	else:
		return True

# If lowThreshold is a number, then lowThreshold is being used,
	# so display this container (displays current lowThreshold).	
	
	import numbers
		
	if isinstance(value,numbers.Number):
		return True
	else:
		return False

# If highThreshold is not a number, then highThreshold is not being used,
	# so display this button (allows user to utilize a highThreshold).	
	
	import numbers
		
	if isinstance(value,numbers.Number):
		return False
	else:
		return True

# If highThreshold is a number, then highThreshold is being used,
	# so display this container (displays current highThreshold).	
	
	import numbers
		
	if isinstance(value,numbers.Number):
		return True
	else:
		return False

messageHandlers.script:
# When the icon is updated via the 'select icon' popup, this
	# handler receives a message containing the icon in the
	# payload; then this updates the current icon.
	
	icon = payload['icon']
	
	self.getChild("Icon").getChild("Icon Cont").custom.icon = icon
